overall.description: First-person cockpit view from an AS350 helicopter flying through dense fog and cloud layers. The footage shows the aircraft instrument panel with occasional faint glimpses of terrain below through thick atmospheric haze. Consistent flight simulator rendering with no other aircraft or objects visible.

scene_state.functional_zone: helicopter cockpit in dense cloud layer

agents.ego_description: piloting the helicopter straight and level through dense instrument meteorological conditions with no external visual references

entered.detail: AS350 helicopter dashboard with artificial horizon, altimeter, and flight instruments visible in lower frame
thick white-gray fog completely obscuring the horizon and surrounding airspace

segments.scene_description: The helicopter maintains steady flight through dense clouds with zero visibility outside the cockpit. The instrument panel remains fixed in the lower frame while the view outside is completely white with occasional faint textures of terrain far below.

scene_state.functional_zone: helicopter cockpit descending through cloud layers

events.description: brief moment of slightly improved visibility allows faint glimpse of ground terrain through thinner cloud layer

agents.ego_description: continuing flight with slight descent through varying cloud densities, maintaining instrument scan

segments.scene_description: The helicopter continues through persistent fog with minor variations in cloud density. The instrument panel remains the primary visual reference as the aircraft encounters brief patches where the ground becomes faintly visible through thinner cloud layers before returning to uniform whiteout conditions.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person perspective from a helicopter cockpit as it navigates through a persistent, dense whiteout. It begins with the aircraft maintaining steady flight through thick clouds where visibility is nearly zero, leaving the instrument panel as the sole fixed point of reference. As the helicopter pushes forward, the uniform white haze fluctuates in density, creating brief moments where the textures of the terrain far below become faintly visible. The scene then shifts back into a heavy fog, with the ground disappearing once more as the aircraft returns to a state of complete atmospheric occlusion.
